sections.heading: Supported Storage Services
sections.body:
Atlas currently supports the following object
storage services:

- AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) buckets

- Azure Blob Storage

- Google Cloud Storage buckets

Additionally, Atlas supports exporting snapshots to AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service)
buckets using PrivateLink.

sections.heading: How Atlas Exports Snapshots
sections.body:
> **Note:** If you export backups to AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service), you can enable
> [AWS S3 Object Lock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/object-lock-configure.html)
> to ensure the immutability of your backup files required
> for compliance and security. Object locks prevent deletions
> and modifications of exported backup files.

You can manually export individual snapshots or set up an export
policy for automatic export of your snapshots. For automatic exports,
you must specify a frequency in your export policy:

- Daily

- Weekly

- Monthly

- Yearly

Atlas automatically exports any backup snapshot with the
frequency type that matches the export frequency. The exported result
is a full backup of that snapshot.

> **Example:** Consider the following:
>
> - A backup policy that sets a weekly and monthly snapshot schedule
>
> - An export policy that sets a monthly export frequency
>
> Suppose, at the end of the month, the weekly and monthly snapshots
> happen on the same day. There would be `4` snapshots of which
> `3` would be weekly snapshots and the fourth snapshot, although
> treated as a weekly snapshot by Atlas, would also be the monthly
> snapshot because it happened on the same day. Atlas will export
> the monthly snapshot only because the export frequency matches the
> snapshot frequency for that snapshot. To export the weekly snapshots
> as well, update the export policy to export weekly snapshots also.
> If the export frequency is set to weekly, Atlas would export all
> `4` snapshots.

As the export progresses, you may see partial results in your object
storage service.

Atlas persists documents in snapshots irrespective of
[Time to Live](manual/tutorial/expire-data) settings. You can access these
documents from your snapshot past their Time to Live deadline.

To learn about snapshot export pricing, see [Snapshot Export Costs](snapshot-export-costs).

sections.heading: Files Atlas Uploads
sections.body:
Atlas uploads an empty file to `/exported_snapshots/.permissioncheck`
when you:* Add a new [object store](snapshot-export-supported-storage) for export

* Start an exportAfter Atlas finishes exporting, Atlas uploads a metadata file
named `.complete` and a metadata file named `metadata.json` for
each collection.**Complete:**.complete FileAtlas uploads the metadata file named `.complete` in the
following path on your object store:```shell
/exported_snapshots/<orgUUID>/<projectUUID>/<clusterName>/<initiationDateOfSnapshot>/<timestamp>/
```> **Note:** By default, Atlas uses organization and project UUIDs in
> the path for the metadata files. To use organization and
> project names instead of UUIDs, set the
> `useOrgAndGroupNamesInExportPrefix` flag to `true` via the
> [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupclusterbackupschedule). Atlas
> replaces any spaces with underscores (`_`) and removes any
> [characters that might require special handling](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/object-keys.html) and
> [characters to avoid](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/object-keys.html) from the
> organization and project names in the path.The `.complete` metadata file is in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format and contains
the following fields:| Field                  | Description                                                                               |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| orgId                  | Unique 24-hexadecimal digit string that identifies the Atlas organization.                |
| orgName                | Name of the Atlas organization.                                                           |
| groupId                | Unique 24-hexadecimal digit string that identifies the project in the Atlas organization. |
| groupName              | Name of the Atlas project.                                                                |
| clusterUniqueId        | Unique 24-hexadecimal digit string that identifies the Atlas cluster.                     |
| clusterName            | Name of the Atlas project.                                                                |
| snapshotInitiationDate | Date when snapshot was taken.                                                             |
| totalFiles             | Total number of files uploaded to the object store.                                       |
| labels                 | Labels of the cluster whose snapshot was exported.                                        |
| customData             | Custom data, if any, that you specified when creating the export job.                     |> **Example:** 
>
> ```json
> {
>   "orgId": "60512d6f65e4047fe0842095",
>   "orgName": "org1",
>   "groupId": "60512dac65e4047fe084220f",
>   "groupName": "group1",
>   "clusterUniqueId": "60512dac65e4047fe0842212",
>   "clusterName": "cluster0",
>   "snapshotInitiationDate": "2020-04-03T05:50:29.321Z"
>   "totalFiles": 23,
>   "labels": [
>     {
>       "key": "key1",
>       "value": "xyz"
>     },
>     {
>       "key": "key2",
>       "value": "xyzuio"
>     }
>   ],
>   "customData": [
>     {
>       "key": "key1",
>       "value": "xyz"
>     },
>     {
>       "key": "key2",
>       "value": "xyzuio"
>     }
>   ]
> }
> ```**Metadata:**metadata.json FileAtlas uploads the `metadata.json` file for each collection
in the following path on your object store:```shell
/exported_snapshots/<orgUUID>/<projectUUID>/<clusterName>/<initiationDateOfSnapshot>/<timestamp>/<dbName>/<collectionName>/metadata.json
```> **Note:** By default, Atlas uses organization and project UUIDs in
> the path for the metadata files. To use organization and
> project names instead of UUIDs, set the
> `useOrgAndGroupNamesInExportPrefix` flag via the
> [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupclusterbackupschedule) to true.
> Atlas replaces any spaces with underscores (`_`) and
> removes any [characters that might require special
> handling](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/object-keys.html) and
> [characters to avoid](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/object-keys.html) from the
> organization and project names in the path.The metadata file is in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format and contains the following
fields:| Field          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| collectionName | Human-readable label that identifies the collection.                                                                                                                                                                        |
| indexes        | List of all the indexes on the collection in the format returned by db.collection.getIndexes command.                                                                                                                       |
| options        | Configuration options defined on the collection. To learn more about the options, see db.createCollection() command.                                                                                                        |
| type           | (Optional) Type of collection. This field is only supported for time series collections, with a value of timeseries. Leave this field unset for standard collections.Atlas doesn't support export of view type collections. |
| uuid           | Collection's UUID. To learn more about UUID, see UUID.                                                                                                                                                                      |> **Example:** 
>
> ```json
> {
>   "options":{
>     "viewOn":"othercol",
>     "pipeline":[{"$project":{"namez":"$name"}}]
>   },
>   "indexes":[],
>   "collectionName":"viewcol",
>   "type":"view"
> }
> ```
>
> ```json
> {
>   "options":{
>     "timeseries":{
>       "timeField":"timestamp",
>       "granularity":"seconds",
>       "bucketMaxSpanSeconds":{"$numberInt":"3600"}
>     }
>   },
>   "indexes":[],
>   "collectionName":"timeseriescol",
>   "type":"timeseries"
> }
> ```
>
> ```json
> {
>   "indexes": [
>     {
>       "v":{"$numberInt":"2"},
>       "key":{
>         "_id":{"$numberInt":"1"}
>       },
>       "name":"_id_"
>     }
>   ],
>   "uuid":"342c40a937c34c478bab03de8ce44f3e",
>   "collectionName":"somecol"
> }
> ```If an export job fails:* Atlas doesn't automatically try to export again.

* Atlas doesn't remove any partial data in your object store.

sections.heading: Exported Data Format
sections.body:
Atlas uploads `gzip`-compressed [Extended JSON (v2)](manual/reference/mongodb-extended-json)
documents. Atlas doesn't upload these documents in order. The following is
the path to the files on your [object store](snapshot-export-supported-storage):

```sh
/exported_snapshots/<orgName>/<projectName>/<clusterName>/<initiationDateOfSnapshot>/<timestamp>/<dbName>/<collectionName>/<shardName>.<increment>.json.gz
```

Where:

| \<orgName>                  | Name of your Atlas organization.                                                         |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| \<projectName>              | Name of your Atlas project.                                                              |
| \<clusterName>              | Name of your Atlas cluster.                                                              |
| \<initiationDateOfSnapshot> | Date when snapshot was taken.                                                            |
| \<timestamp>                | Timestamp when the export job was created.                                               |
| \<dbName>                   | Name of the database in the Atlas cluster.                                               |
| \<collectionName>           | Name of the Atlas collection.                                                            |
| \<shardName>                | Name of the replica set. For sharded collections, this is the name of the primary shard. |
| \<increment>                | Count that is incremented as chunks are uploaded. Starts at 0.                           |

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
You can't perform the following actions:

- Export [fallback snapshots](cps-fallback-snapshots).

- Have more than one active export per snapshot.

- Export [view collections](manual/core/views), or
  [system collections](manual/reference/system-collections),
  with the exception of `<database>.system.js` collections.

- Export snapshots from clusters in an Atlas project
  with IP-restricted [Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management](security-kms-encryption) enabled.

- Export snapshots from clusters hosted on Azure (Microsoft Azure) or Google Cloud Storage
  in projects configured for
  [Private Endpoint](atlas-configure-private-endpoint).

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To manage your Cloud Backup snapshots, you must have
[Project Backup Manager](<Project Backup Manager>) or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access
to the project.

Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves as
a `Project Backup Manager` or `Project Owner` to the project before
they can manage Cloud Backup snapshots.

To download and export Cloud Backup snapshots, you must have
[Project Backup Export Operator](<Project Backup Export Operator>) access to the project.

sections.heading: Export Management
sections.body:
<SelectedContent>
  # Prerequisites

  To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
  higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
  you must do the following:

  - Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_use.html)
    with `STS:AssumeRole` that grants Atlas access to your AWS (Amazon Web Services)
    resources. To learn more about configuring AWS (Amazon Web Services) access for
    Atlas, see [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

  - Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)
    that grants Atlas write access or the `S3:PutObject` and
    `S3:GetBucketLocation` permissions to your AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources. To
    learn more about configuring write access to AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources, see
    [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

    > **Example:** 
    >
    > ```json
    > {
    >   "Version": "2012-10-17",
    >   "Statement": [
    >     {
    >       "Effect": "Allow",
    >       "Action": "s3:GetBucketLocation",
    >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name"
    >     },
    >     {
    >       "Effect": "Allow",
    >       "Action": "s3:PutObject",
    >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name/*"
    >     }
    >   ]
    > }
    > ```

  # Export Job Management

  ## Create an Export Job

  To create a snapshot export job, use the [atlas api
  cloudBackups createBackupExport](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-createBackupExport) command.

  ## View Export Jobs

  To view an existing snapshot export job, use the [atlas api
  cloudBackups listBackupExports](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-listBackupExports) command.

  ## Create One Export Bucket

  To create a bucket for snapshot export, use the [atlas api
  cloudBackups createExportBucket](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-createExportBucket) command.

  ## View Export Buckets

  To view a single existing snapshot export bucket, use the
  [atlas api cloudBackups getExportBucket](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-getExportBucket) command.

  Or, to view a list of all existing snapshot export buckets within the
  current project, use the [atlas api cloudBackups
  listExportBuckets](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-listExportBuckets) command.

  ## Delete an Export Bucket

  To delete an existing snapshot export bucket, use the [atlas
  api cloudBackups deleteExportBuckets](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-deleteExportBuckets) command.
</SelectedContent>

To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_use.html)
  with `STS:AssumeRole` that grants Atlas access to your AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  resources. To learn more about configuring AWS (Amazon Web Services) access for
  Atlas, see [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

- Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)
  that grants Atlas write access or the `S3:PutObject` and
  `S3:GetBucketLocation` permissions to your AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources. To
  learn more about configuring write access to AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources, see
  [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

  > **Example:** 
  >
  > ```json
  > {
  >   "Version": "2012-10-17",
  >   "Statement": [
  >     {
  >       "Effect": "Allow",
  >       "Action": "s3:GetBucketLocation",
  >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name"
  >     },
  >     {
  >       "Effect": "Allow",
  >       "Action": "s3:PutObject",
  >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name/*"
  >     }
  >   ]
  > }
  > ```

To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_use.html)
  with `STS:AssumeRole` that grants Atlas access to your AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  resources. To learn more about configuring AWS (Amazon Web Services) access for
  Atlas, see [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

- Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)
  that grants Atlas write access or the `S3:PutObject` and
  `S3:GetBucketLocation` permissions to your AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources. To
  learn more about configuring write access to AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources, see
  [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

  > **Example:** 
  >
  > ```json
  > {
  >   "Version": "2012-10-17",
  >   "Statement": [
  >     {
  >       "Effect": "Allow",
  >       "Action": "s3:GetBucketLocation",
  >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name"
  >     },
  >     {
  >       "Effect": "Allow",
  >       "Action": "s3:PutObject",
  >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name/*"
  >     }
  >   ]
  > }
  > ```

To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- Set up Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Set Up and Manage Azure Service Principal Access](manage-azure-access) for your Atlas project.

- Assign the *Storage Blob Delegator* and *Storage Blob Data
  Contributor* roles to your Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Service Principal](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/powershell/azure/create-azure-service-principal-azureps).

  To assign the roles to your Service Principal, you will need
  the following information:

  - [Service Principal ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/how-to-view-managed-identity-service-principal?pivots=identity-mi-service-principal-portal)

  - [Subscription ID](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-portal/get-subscription-tenant-id)

  - [Resource Group Name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/manage-resource-groups-portal)

  - [Storage Account Name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-get-info)

  - [Container Name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/blob-containers-portal)

  | Role                          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
  | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Storage Blob Delegator        | This allows the Service Principal to sign SAS tokens to access the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Storage Container. To assign this role, run the following command:az role assignment create --assignee-object-id \<service-principal-id> --role "Storage Blob Delegator" --scope /subscriptions/\<subscription-id>/resourceGroups/\<resource-group-name>/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/\<storage-account-name>                                                                                          |
  | Storage Blob Data Contributor | This allows read, write, and delete blob access for the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Storage Container. To assign this role, run the following command:az role assignment create --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal --assignee-object-id \<service-principal-id> --role "Storage Blob Data Contributor" --scope /subscriptions/\<subscription-id>/resourceGroups/\<resource-group-name>/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/\<storage-account-name>/blobServices/default/containers/\<container-name> |

sections.heading: Create an Export Job
sections.body:
To create a snapshot export job, use the [atlas api
cloudBackups createBackupExport](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-createBackupExport) command.

sections.heading: View Export Jobs
sections.body:
To view an existing snapshot export job, use the [atlas api
cloudBackups listBackupExports](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-listBackupExports) command.

sections.heading: Create One Export Bucket
sections.body:
To create a bucket for snapshot export, use the [atlas api
cloudBackups createExportBucket](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-createExportBucket) command.

sections.heading: View Export Buckets
sections.body:
To view a single existing snapshot export bucket, use the
[atlas api cloudBackups getExportBucket](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-getExportBucket) command.

Or, to view a list of all existing snapshot export buckets within the
current project, use the [atlas api cloudBackups
listExportBuckets](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-listExportBuckets) command.

sections.heading: Delete an Export Bucket
sections.body:
To delete an existing snapshot export bucket, use the [atlas
api cloudBackups deleteExportBuckets](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-deleteExportBuckets) command.

To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- Set up Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Set Up and Manage Azure Service Principal Access](manage-azure-access) for your Atlas project.

- Assign the *Storage Blob Delegator* and *Storage Blob Data
  Contributor* roles to your Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Service Principal](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/powershell/azure/create-azure-service-principal-azureps).

  To assign the roles to your Service Principal, you will need
  the following information:

  - [Service Principal ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/how-to-view-managed-identity-service-principal?pivots=identity-mi-service-principal-portal)

  - [Subscription ID](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-portal/get-subscription-tenant-id)

  - [Resource Group Name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/manage-resource-groups-portal)

  - [Storage Account Name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-get-info)

  - [Container Name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/blob-containers-portal)

  | Role                          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
  | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Storage Blob Delegator        | This allows the Service Principal to sign SAS tokens to access the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Storage Container. To assign this role, run the following command:az role assignment create --assignee-object-id \<service-principal-id> --role "Storage Blob Delegator" --scope /subscriptions/\<subscription-id>/resourceGroups/\<resource-group-name>/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/\<storage-account-name>                                                                                          |
  | Storage Blob Data Contributor | This allows read, write, and delete blob access for the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Storage Container. To assign this role, run the following command:az role assignment create --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal --assignee-object-id \<service-principal-id> --role "Storage Blob Data Contributor" --scope /subscriptions/\<subscription-id>/resourceGroups/\<resource-group-name>/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/\<storage-account-name>/blobServices/default/containers/\<container-name> |

To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- [Manage GCP Access](manage-gcp-access).

- Locate the ID of your Google Cloud service account in Atlas by following the
  steps in [View Authorized Google Cloud Service Accounts](view-gcpservice-principal).

- In your Google Cloud console, add the service account ID from the previous step
  as a principal to your
  bucket's policy by following the [Google Cloud documentation](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/using-iam-permissions#console).

  This grants the [Storage Object User](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam-roles#storage.objectUser) role to the service account
  for your specific Google Cloud Storage bucket.

sections.body:
To create a snapshot export job, use the [atlas api
cloudBackups createBackupExport](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-createBackupExport) command.

Or, to view a list of all existing snapshot export buckets within the
current project, use the [atlas api cloudBackups

sections.heading: Delete an Export Bucket
sections.body:
To delete an existing snapshot export bucket, use the [atlas
api cloudBackups deleteExportBuckets](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-cloudBackups-deleteExportBuckets) command.

To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- [Manage GCP Access](manage-gcp-access).

- Locate the ID of your Google Cloud service account in Atlas by following the
  steps in [View Authorized Google Cloud Service Accounts](view-gcpservice-principal).

- In your Google Cloud console, add the service account ID from the previous step
  as a principal to your
  bucket's policy by following the [Google Cloud documentation](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/using-iam-permissions#console).

  This grants the [Storage Object User](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam-roles#storage.objectUser) role to the service account
  for your specific Google Cloud Storage bucket.

To grant and manage cloud provider access and to create and manage
snapshot export jobs, the API (Application Programming Interface) that you use must have the
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) role.

sections.heading: Create One Export Bucket
sections.body:
To grant access to an object store for exporting snapshots, send
a `POST` request to the Cloud Backups
[endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupbackupexportbucket). This enables the object store
to receive Atlas Cloud Backup snapshots. When sending the
request to grant access, you must provide the following
information:

- Unique 24-hexadecimal character string that identifies the
  unified AWS access role ID that Atlas must use to
  access the AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) Bucket. To learn more, see
  [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

sections.heading: Create One PrivateLink Export Bucket
sections.body:
To enable snapshot export over an AWS (Amazon Web Services) Private Link connection,
perform the following procedure:

Create an AWS (Amazon Web Services) private endpoint by sending a `POST`
request to the Cloud Backups [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupbackupprivateendpoint).

You must create the private endpoint in the same region as both
the cluster from which you source snapshots and the S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket
to which you want to export snapshots. Atlas doesn't
support cross-region snapshot export over a private link
connection.

Create an export bucket by sending a `POST` request to
the Cloud Backups [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupbackupexportbucket). To enable this bucket for
PrivateLink export, you must set the
`requirePrivateNetworking` field to `true`.

```javascript
{
  "cloudProvider": "AWS",
  "iamRoleId": "79aa4210be5b28e406c3c305",
  "bucketName": "exemplary-bucket",
  "requirePrivateNetworking": true
}
```

sections.heading: Update One Export Bucket to a PrivateLink Bucket
sections.body:
IMPORTANT: This feature is only available for AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service)
export buckets.

To update the `requirePrivateNetworking` value of an existing
AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket to which Atlas exports snapshots, send a
`PATCH` request to the [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupbackupexportbucket):

- Unique 24-hexadecimal character string that identifies the
  Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal that Atlas must use to
  access the Azure Blob Storage. To learn more, see
  [Set Up and Manage Azure Service Principal Access](manage-azure-access).

- Service endpoint of your Azure Blob Storage account. To learn more,
  see [Azure Documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-get-info?tabs=azure-cli#get-service-endpoints-for-the-storage-account).

* Unique 24-hexadecimal digit string that identifies the Google Cloud Provider
  Access Role that Atlas uses to access the Google Cloud Storage bucket.

sections.heading: List All Export Buckets
sections.body:
To retrieve all the AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) Buckets, Azure Blob Storage Containers, or
Google Cloud Storage buckets to which Atlas exports snapshots, send a
`GET` request to the Cloud Backups
[endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupbackupexportbuckets).

sections.heading: Delete One Export Bucket
sections.body:
To delete an Export Bucket, you must first disable automatic
export of snapshots to the AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) Bucket, Azure Blob Storage
Container, or Google Cloud Storage bucket for all clusters in the project
and then send a `DELETE` request to the Cloud Backups
[endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deletegroupbackupexportbucket)
with the ID of the Export Bucket. If necessary, send a `GET`
request to the [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupbackupexportbuckets) to retrieve the export Bucket
ID.

sections.heading: Create Snapshot Export Job
sections.body:
To export one Atlas backup snapshot to an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) Bucket,
Azure Blob Storage Container, or Google Cloud Storage bucket, send a `POST` request
to the Cloud Backups [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupclusterbackupexport) with the ID of the snapshot to
export and the ID of the AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) Bucket, Azure Blob Storage Container,
or Google Cloud Storage bucket.

sections.heading: Retrieve Snapshot Export Job
sections.body:
To retrieve one snapshot export job by its ID, send a `GET`
request to the Cloud Backups [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupclusterbackupexport) with the ID of the export job.

To retrieve all running snapshot export jobs, send a `GET`
request to the Cloud Backups [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupclusterbackupexports).
